title: Constellation card — reserved + cross-type metadata keys
description: Keys every card may use, regardless of type. The first four are the reserved keys (name, kind, status, connections); the rest are optional cross-type metadata managed by tooling (code binding + verification provenance + durable notes). All other frontmatter keys are type-specific fields defined in the per-type schemas.

name.description: Display name. The handle (filename) is the identity; this is the human label.

kind.description: Subtype discriminator, lowercase slug (e.g. sql-table, e2e, decision).

status.description: Lifecycle state. Orthogonal to git history: 'what changed' comes from git, not from this field.

connections.description: Plain list of handles this card is connected to. Undirected; declare on whichever card you are editing.

items.description: Repo-relative path, optionally path:symbol (e.g. src/api/tickets.ts or src/api/tickets.ts:createTicket).

code_refs.description: Code this card is bound to, for drift detection and code attach. Optional precision binding ON ANY card; the primary binding stays a connected FILE card's path:. Symbol after a colon is an informational hint — drift is detected at file granularity.

verified_sha.description: Git sha this card's claim was last verified against (set by set_verified). The baseline for code-side drift detection — not a change-tracking flag (that is git's job). The staleness VERDICT is always recomputed live and never stored.

verified_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the card was last verified. Provenance of the verified claim, not a changelog of the card.

kind.description: decision = a choice + why; gotcha = a non-obvious trap; state = current built/live reality; deviation = where code intentionally differs from the card; verified = a verification note.

text.description: The note body. Capture what code can't say; do not paste code that lives in the repo.

sha.description: Optional git sha the note was recorded against.

notes.description: Append-only typed memory the code can't hold (decisions, gotchas, current state, deviations). Ordered newest-last by append order; managed by append_note. Queryable by kind. No timestamps — ordering is array position; 'what changed when' is git's job.
